location.origin: Seattle, Washington -- born 1979; native-born, Generation X; studied at University of Washington jazz studies program under Marc Seales (degree 13); completed studies locally, remained in Seattle
location.formative: University of Washington (under Seales); Seattle jazz scene (Jazz Alley, Tula's, Royal Room, Earshot Jazz Festival); Origin Records partnership; performances with Maynard Ferguson, Benny Golson
location.career: Seattle, Washington (permanent base) -- Origin Records (Flexicon 2006, Human 2008, Dialogue 2010, Urban Folklore 2013); one of Seattle's most active jazz trumpeters; chose to stay and build locally rather than export to NYC

theory_nodes.concept: Hard bop trumpet vocabulary (Clifford Brown lineage -- bebop phrases at brass tempo, post-bop harmonic language -- altered dominants and tritone subs as standard equipment, the Origin Records documentary realism aesthetic, swing-to-even-eighths rhythmic continuum)
examples: Thomas Marriott -- Flexicon (Origin, 2006): hard bop trumpet vocabulary, crisp articulation, melodic invention within standard harmonic frameworks, rhythmic drive of a working band
Thomas Marriott -- Human (Origin, 2008): extended palette toward lyrical open playing, ballad expressiveness, dynamic control
Thomas Marriott -- Dialogue (Origin, 2010): interactive group dynamics, conversational post-bop ensemble playing
Thomas Marriott -- Urban Folklore (Origin, 2013): jazz vocabulary connected to urban experience, city-embedded hard bop
Clifford Brown -- Study in Brown: the lineage source for hard bop trumpet technique; bebop phrases at brass tempo with greater harmonic clarity
Freddie Hubbard -- Red Clay: hard bop trumpet extended into post-bop and jazz-funk territory
Woody Shaw -- Rosewood: advanced post-bop trumpet harmony with pentatonic superimposition
theory_nodes.description: Hard bop trumpet vocabulary in the Clifford Brown lineage is the most direct inheritance of the bebop revolution (degree 12, Lanphere) filtered through the hard bop transformation of the late 1950s. Core techniques: (1) Clifford Brown lineage -- rapid chromatic runs, arpeggiated chord tones at tempo, physical endurance for multi-chorus solos; Brown demonstrated that bebop's chromatic vocabulary (previously saxophone-associated) could execute on trumpet at comparable speed. (2) Post-bop harmonic language -- altered dominant chords (b9, #9, #11, b13), tritone substitutions deployed as standard vocabulary (inherited from modal revolution, degrees 13-14), upper-structure triads over dominant chords, pentatonic superimposition over complex changes. (3) The Origin Records documentary realism aesthetic -- clean recordings, natural acoustic space, minimal post-production; a third position distinct from ECM (spacious, reverberant) and Blue Note/Van Gelder (warm compression). The recording captures the ensemble as it sounds in the room. (4) Swing-to-even-eighths rhythmic continuum -- predominantly swing-based but flexible enough to straighten eighth notes when required; the contemporary hard bop player navigates standards (swing), originals (variable), and modern compositions (even eighths). Fifteenth chromaticism deployment: hard bop trumpet chromaticism in the Brown lineage.

ancestors: Bebop vocabulary -- chromatic approach, tritone substitution (degree 12, Lanphere)
Hard bop trumpet -- Clifford Brown, Freddie Hubbard, Woody Shaw (national lineage)
Swing/bebop trumpet -- Standifer (degree 4, first trumpet in engine)
Post-bop harmonic language -- inherited from modal revolution (degrees 13-14)
Origin Records production aesthetic -- John Bishop, Seattle jazz documentation
genealogy.relationship: Hard bop trumpet descends from bebop (degree 12) through the specific brass lineage of Clifford Brown, who translated Parker's saxophone vocabulary to trumpet. Marriott inherits this through two generations of post-Brown trumpeters (Hubbard, Shaw) and adds post-bop harmonic extensions. The brass theory branch extends to four nodes at degree 15 (Standifer's multi-instrumental trumpet at 4, plus additional brass nodes, plus Marriott's specialist hard bop trumpet). The fifteenth chromaticism deployment: hard bop trumpet chromaticism at energy-3, assertive and projective. The sixteenth tritone substitution deployment: professional standard equipment in the hard bop trumpet context.

theory_nodes.concept: The Builder-Stayer archetype (national-level ability + conscious choice to remain in origin city + active investment in local infrastructure via Origin Records + scene-mediated stay; generational complement to The Persistence at degree 5)
examples: Thomas Marriott (degree 15) -- national credentials (Ferguson, Golson) + conscious Seattle choice + Origin Records partnership + local venue circuit; the Builder-Stayer
Overton Berry (degree 5) -- The Persistence: stayed because the scene was self-sustaining in the pre-institutional era; passive retention (comparison)
Marc Seales (degree 13) -- The Institutional Anchor: stays and teaches within the university system; institution-mediated stay (comparison)
Aaron Parks (degree 14) -- The Prodigy-Export: exported at 16 through institutional pipeline; opposite choice from same UW pipeline (comparison)
Floyd Standifer (degree 4) -- The Teacher: stayed and taught experientially; bandstand mentorship (comparison)
theory_nodes.description: The Builder-Stayer is the sixteenth named pattern in the artist-city taxonomy. It describes a musician with national-level ability who consciously chooses to remain in the origin city and invest in local infrastructure rather than export to a career center. Four conditions: (1) National-level ability demonstrated through performances with nationally recognized artists. (2) Conscious choice to remain in origin city -- not inability to leave but ability plus choice. (3) Active investment in local infrastructure -- recording for local label, performing at local venues, building relationships with local institutions. (4) Career based locally, not as satellite. The Builder-Stayer is the generational complement to The Persistence (Berry, degree 5). Berry stayed because there was nowhere else to go. Marriott stays because there is somewhere else to go (NYC, LA) and he chooses not to. The Persistence is passive retention; the Builder-Stayer is active investment. Same outcome, different mechanism, different agency.

ancestors: The Persistence -- Berry (degree 5); stayed because scene was the world
The Teacher -- Standifer (degree 4); stayed and taught experientially
The Institutional Anchor -- Seales (degree 13); stays and teaches within university
The Prodigy-Export -- Parks (degree 14); exported through institutional pipeline
genealogy.relationship: Sixteenth pattern in sixteen degrees. The Builder-Stayer completes a three-degree UW pipeline sequence: Seales (13, Institutional Anchor -- stays and teaches), Parks (14, Prodigy-Export -- leaves), Marriott (15, Builder-Stayer -- stays and builds). Three consecutive UW-connected degrees, three structurally different outcomes from the same pipeline. The stayed-vs-left question is the central tension of Seattle jazz: the city produces talent, but can it keep it? Two stay (institution-mediated and scene-mediated), one leaves. The same UW pipeline produces all three.

theory_nodes.concept: Origin Records as local infrastructure (Seattle-based independent jazz label, founded John Bishop 1997, documentary realism production aesthetic, the first label in the engine where both artist and label are Seattle-based and Seattle-committed)
examples: Origin Records -- founded John Bishop, 1997, Seattle; dedicated to documenting Pacific Northwest jazz community; clean recordings, natural acoustic space, minimal post-production
Thomas Marriott -- Flexicon, Human, Dialogue, Urban Folklore (Origin): the Marriott-Origin partnership as model of artist-label symbiosis in a local scene
Blue Note Records (degrees 12, 14) -- national institution (comparison); the most storied label in jazz history
ECM Records (degrees 1, 7, 8, 14) -- European aesthetic label (comparison); Manfred Eicher's spacious production philosophy
Columbia Records (degree 6) -- major label (comparison); national distribution, Bown's sole album
theory_nodes.description: Origin Records is Seattle's premier independent jazz label, founded by drummer John Bishop in 1997. It represents a specific model of jazz infrastructure: a local label dedicated to documenting the local community rather than competing in the national market. The Origin Records production aesthetic -- documentary realism -- is a third position distinct from ECM (spacious, reverberant, contemplative) and Blue Note/Van Gelder (warm compression, studio character). The Origin sound captures the ensemble as it sounds in the room, without imposing an external sonic signature. The label serves the scene rather than defining it. The Marriott-Origin relationship is the first artist-label partnership in the engine where both artist and label are Seattle-based and Seattle-committed. This is label as local infrastructure, not label as career launcher (Blue Note for Parks) or label as aesthetic statement (ECM for Frisell/Peacock).

genealogy.relationship: Origin Records enters the engine as the first explicitly local institutional label -- a label that is itself infrastructure for the local scene. Prior local recordings (Standifer 4, Berry 5, Brazil 11) were on undocumented or minimal labels. Origin Records is a deliberate institution dedicated to Pacific Northwest jazz documentation. The label threads expand: Mercury (0, 3), Columbia (6), ECM (1, 7, 8, 14), Catalyst/Mainstream (9), Pacific Jazz (10), local (4, 5, 11), Fresh Sound (12), Blue Note (12, 14), Naim (13), Ropeadope (14), Origin (15 -- NEW). Origin is the first local-institutional label.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- assertive brass (fifteenth deployment; half-valve bend, crushed grace note, scooped approach from below; brass-specific technique at energy-3 -- assertive and driving, not warm and educational)
examples: Thomas Marriott -- half-valve bend, crushed grace note, scooped approach from below; physical manipulation of the instrument producing microtonal shadings unavailable on piano or guitar; energy-3 assertive deployment
Floyd Standifer (degree 4) -- warm brass blue note at energy-2; educational warmth; the first brass-specific deployment
Clifford Brown -- precise, clean blue note execution at tempo; the lineage source for hard bop brass blue note technique
Miles Davis -- muted half-valve blue note; the most famous brass blue note deployment in jazz history
Marc Seales (degree 13) -- modal integration where blue notes become defining pitches of the mode; the predecessor deployment
theory_nodes.description: Fifteenth deployment of the blue note thread. In hard bop trumpet, the blue note (b3, b7) is a declarative gesture executed through brass-specific physical technique: half-valve bends (partially depressing valves to produce microtonal pitch bends), crushed grace notes (rapid ornamental notes leading into the target pitch), and scooped approaches from below (sliding up to the target pitch from a lower starting point). These techniques produce microtonal shadings unavailable on fixed-pitch instruments like piano or guitar. At energy-3, the deployment is assertive and driving -- the blue note commands rather than comforts. This is the second brass-specific blue note deployment after Standifer (degree 4), but at a full energy level higher. Standifer's blue note was warm and educational (energy-2); Marriott's is assertive and projective (energy-3). Same instrument, same technique, different energy -- the trumpet's blue note has intensified across the arc.

ancestors: Blue note -- warm brass trumpet (degree 4, Standifer)
Blue note -- all fourteen prior deployments (degrees 0-14)
Clifford Brown blue note technique -- the hard bop lineage source
Miles Davis half-valve technique -- the most famous brass blue note
genealogy.relationship: Fifteen deployments across sixteen degrees. The assertive brass blue note is the highest-energy deployment to date (energy-3). Extends the brass-specific pair: Standifer (4, energy-2 warm) and Marriott (15, energy-3 assertive). Fifteen deployments: bass-line (0), guitar (1), vocal (3), trumpet-warm (4), piano crush (5), altered voicings (6), trombone slide (7), bass fundamental (8), sax voice (9), alto cool bend (10), flute bent note (11), bebop chromatic approach (12), modal integration (13), post-genre floating (14), assertive brass (15).

seattle_connection: Born in Seattle, 1979 -- native-born musician who studied at UW under Marc Seales (degree 13) and made a conscious choice to stay and build locally. One of Seattle's most active jazz trumpeters. Records for Origin Records (Seattle's premier independent jazz label, founded John Bishop 1997). Performs at Jazz Alley, Tula's, the Royal Room, Earshot Jazz Festival. Played with Maynard Ferguson and Benny Golson (national credentials) but chose Seattle. The Builder-Stayer pattern: national ability, conscious local investment, scene-mediated stay. Third UW pipeline product (after Seales 13 and Parks 14). First energy-3 artist in the engine -- assertive, projective, driving brass energy. The trumpet arc: Standifer (4, energy 2, multi-instrumental) to Marriott (15, energy 3, trumpet-focused). The generational complement to Berry's Persistence (degree 5): Berry stayed because the scene was the world; Marriott stays because he chose to.

theory_genealogy: Hard bop trumpet vocabulary (Clifford Brown lineage, post-bop harmonic language, Origin Records documentary realism, swing-to-even-eighths continuum) -- fourth node on brass theory branch; inherits from bebop (degree 12) through Brown/Hubbard/Shaw; fifteenth chromaticism deployment

The Builder-Stayer archetype (national ability + conscious local investment + scene-mediated stay) -- sixteenth artist-city pattern; complements The Persistence (degree 5) and The Institutional Anchor (degree 13); scene-mediated vs. institution-mediated stay

Origin Records as local infrastructure (Seattle-based independent label, documentary realism aesthetic, artist-label partnership as local scene investment) -- first explicitly local institutional label in the engine; third production aesthetic position (after ECM and Blue Note/Van Gelder)

Blue note -- fifteenth deployment; assertive brass at energy-3 (half-valve bend, crushed grace note, scooped approach); highest-energy blue note deployment; extends brass pair from Standifer (4, warm)

Classify every degree.
Educator spectrum: seven modes (institutional, bandstand, self-taught, performance, salon, elder-as-living-archive, university scholar-performer). Marriott is primarily a performer.
Composition-improvisation spectrum: ten positions stable. Marriott occupies the hard bop center -- standards-based improvisation with original composition.
Rhythmic spectrum: eleven positions (swing-to-even-eighths continuum added at degree 15).

key_albums.title: Flexicon
key_albums.label: Origin Records
key_albums.status: hard bop trumpet debut on Origin Records
key_albums.significance: First Origin Records appearance in the engine. Hard bop trumpet vocabulary: crisp articulation, melodic invention within standard harmonic frameworks, rhythmic drive of a working band. Established Marriott as one of Seattle's leading young jazz trumpeters. Origin Records -- Seattle's premier independent jazz label (John Bishop, 1997). The first artist-label partnership in the engine where both are Seattle-based and Seattle-committed.

key_albums.title: Human
key_albums.label: Origin Records
key_albums.significance: Demonstrated range beyond pure hard bop vocabulary. Expressive ballad playing, dynamic control, the full emotional spectrum of the trumpet. Continued the Marriott-Origin partnership documenting Seattle jazz.

key_albums.title: Dialogue
key_albums.label: Origin Records
key_albums.significance: Emphasized the conversational model of post-bop ensemble playing -- every musician shapes direction in real time. Title reflects approach: jazz as dialogue, not soloist-with-accompaniment. The Builder-Stayer ethos in ensemble form.

key_albums.title: Urban Folklore
key_albums.label: Origin Records
key_albums.status: jazz vocabulary connected to urban experience
key_albums.significance: Connected hard bop vocabulary to the lived reality of a musician embedded in a specific city. The title suggests the Builder-Stayer ethos: urban lore, city stories, Seattle as subject matter for jazz composition. Over a decade of consistent Origin Records output.

degree_neighbors.name: Floyd Standifer
degree_neighbors.relationship: Trumpet lineage. Second trumpeter after Standifer. Elder-to-contemporary at the same instrument. Energy differential: Standifer at 2 (educational warmth), Marriott at 3 (assertive projection). Specialization trend: Standifer multi-instrumental, Marriott trumpet-focused. The trumpet in Seattle jazz has gained a full energy level across the arc.

degree_neighbors.name: Marc Seales
degree_neighbors.relationship: UW teacher-student. Seales directs UW jazz studies; Marriott studied under Seales. Third UW pipeline product. The Institutional Anchor trained the Builder-Stayer. Three consecutive UW-connected degrees (13, 14, 15).

degree_neighbors.name: Aaron Parks
degree_neighbors.relationship: Generational contrast -- stayed vs. left. Both UW pipeline products. Parks exported at 16 (Prodigy-Export); Marriott stayed and built (Builder-Stayer). Near-generational peers (1979 vs. 1983) who made opposite choices. Hard bop vs. post-genre. Origin Records vs. Blue Note/ECM.

degree_neighbors.name: Overton Berry
degree_neighbors.relationship: Persistence parallel. Berry stayed because the scene was the world (passive retention). Marriott stays because he chose to (active investment). Same outcome, different agency. Both are anchors for their respective eras.

garfield_thread.degree_0: Quincy Jones -- attended late 1940s, class of 1950. Arranger/producer. Left Seattle.
garfield_thread.degree_3: Ernestine Anderson -- attended late 1940s, same era as Jones. Vocalist. Left and returned.
garfield_thread.degree_4: Floyd Standifer -- Central District post-WWII, educational path not confirmed as Garfield. Multi-instrumentalist and educator. Never left.
garfield_thread.degree_5: Overton Berry -- Central District native, self-taught. No institutional music education. Autodidact path.
garfield_thread.degree_6: Patti Bown -- Central District native, Seattle University (not Garfield). Formal training via Jesuit university.
garfield_thread.degree_7: Julian Priester -- NOT a Garfield product. DuSable High School (Chicago) under Captain Dyett. Cornish College of the Arts faculty.
garfield_thread.degree_8: Gary Peacock -- NOT a Garfield product. Burley, Idaho. Westlake College (LA). U.S. Army (Germany).
garfield_thread.degree_9: Hadley Caliman -- NOT a Garfield product. Idabel, Oklahoma. Los Angeles jazz scene. Cornish College of the Arts faculty.
garfield_thread.degree_10: Bud Shank -- NOT a Garfield product. Dayton, Ohio. University of North Carolina. Stan Kenton Orchestra. Los Angeles / Pacific Jazz.
garfield_thread.degree_11: Joe Brazil -- NOT a Garfield product. Detroit, Michigan. Detroit jazz scene. Settled in Central District as salon-model educator.
garfield_thread.degree_12: Don Lanphere -- NOT a Garfield product. Wenatchee, Washington. Northwestern University. NYC 52nd Street bebop scene.
garfield_thread.degree_13: Marc Seales -- NOT confirmed as Garfield product. Seattle native. University of Washington (DMA). Seventh consecutive non-Garfield degree. First UW-complete pipeline.
garfield_thread.degree_14: Aaron Parks -- NOT confirmed as Garfield product. Born Seattle 1983. UW pre-college at 14, MSM at 16. Eighth consecutive non-Garfield degree. Exported before completing high school in Seattle.
garfield_thread.degree_15: Thomas Marriott -- NOT confirmed as Garfield product. Born Seattle 1979. UW jazz studies under Seales. Ninth consecutive non-Garfield degree. Stayed in Seattle, built career locally via Origin Records.

cultural_patterns.institution_type: the builder-stayer -- native-born, trained at UW under Seales, national credentials (Ferguson, Golson), conscious choice to remain in Seattle, active investment in local infrastructure via Origin Records; scene-mediated stay, not institution-mediated; the generational complement to Berry's Persistence (degree 5)
